title: AI-native Virtual Chiplet Eco-systems: Shift Left, Shift Up, and Shift Out to accelerate Chiplet adoption - Semiwiki
gist: Raghu Shankar argues chiplet/SiP adoption is bottlenecked at the front-end planning phase, not the technology, and proposes an AI-native multi-vendor 'virtual chiplet ecosystem' (shift-left, shift-up, shift-out) under OCP's Open Chiplet Economy to de-risk early multi-vendor design exploration.

insights.claim: Systems-in-package using 2.5D/3D heterogeneous integration of multiple dies and chiplets deliver roughly 10x more functionality than traditional monolithic chips across scientific computing, automotive, edge, and aerospace/defense.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: Frames the upside case for advanced packaging demand; watch packaging/HI capacity at foundries and OSATs as the functionality-per-package gap widens vs. monolithic.
insights.quote: Systems-in-package (SIPs) with 2.5D and 3D heterogenous integration, consisting of multiple dies and chiplets deliver 10x more functionality than traditional monolithic chips.

insights.claim: Chiplet adoption so far has been confined to established vendors using proven legacy designs in well-understood markets and closed ecosystems of few vendors, leaving broad multi-vendor, lower-volume, lower-budget adoption unrealized.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: The TAM expansion for chiplets depends on opening closed ecosystems; standards/interop enablers (UCIe, OCP) are the unlock to watch.

insights.claim: The primary barrier to broad chiplet adoption is a front-end planning/design bottleneck in identifying which ventures are worth pursuing, not the manufacturing technology itself.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: Tooling that compresses front-end multi-vendor evaluation (EDA, HLS, design-space exploration) is where incremental value accrues, not just packaging.
insights.quote: There is a bottleneck right up-front during planning and design phase to identify promising ventures.
insights.locator: Despite the advantages of SIPs and chiplets...

insights.claim: Shankar proposes solving chiplet adoption via three moves: shift-left (move multi-vendor evaluation to the earliest planning/PPAC phase), shift-up (abstract to the pre-RTL architecture layer for faster interoperability testing), and shift-out (break industry silos to expand the pool of IP, buyers, and sellers).
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: A reusable mental model for evaluating where EDA/IP vendors add value in chiplet design flows; pre-RTL architectural exploration is the contested layer.
insights.quote: This requires a shift-up: Scope it down to architecture layer for faster interoperability testing.

insights.claim: Evaluating chiplet design options must scale to thousands of opportunities from which only a handful advance, demanding low-cost, low-friction, multi-organization exploration that diverse stakeholders agree on.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: Scale economics favor automated/AI-driven screening tools over bespoke evaluation; a structural tailwind for design-automation software.
insights.quote: this evaluation needs to be scaled to 1000’s of opportunities from which a handful will make the cut for further investigation and investment.
insights.locator: The above needs to be solved in a low-risk, low cost...

insights.claim: Shankar calls for an AI-native multi-vendor virtual chiplet ecosystem containing specs, behavioral models, curated stimuli, reference designs, and interop verification methodologies, with AI applied to semantic/keyword spec search, match scoring, gap detection, code generation, and EDA/testbench connections.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: An emerging category at the AI + EDA intersection; watch EDA majors and IP vendors for AI-native design-exploration offerings.
insights.quote: we need to define a robust, AI native multi-vendor virtual chiplet eco-system for planning and prototyping phases.
insights.locator: As an industry we need to define...

insights.claim: The Open Chiplet Economy (OCE) under the Open Compute Project (OCP) is positioned as the vendor-neutral body driving this multi-vendor chiplet prototyping vision, drawing commercial, research-lab, and academic participants.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: OCP/OCE is the standards venue to monitor for chiplet interoperability momentum and which vendors align early.
insights.quote: Open chiplet economy (OCE) under Open Compute Project (OCP) is a vendor-neutral body encouraging participation from commercial, research labs, and academia

insights.claim: Shankar flags memory as a 'valuable commodity now,' implying memory utilization is a binding constraint that heterogeneous multi-die solutions must optimize around.
insights.speaker: raghu shankar
insights.so_what: Corroborates the broader memory-scarcity theme; architectural designs that maximize memory utilization gain relative value during a memory crunch.
